note: contrived test word (genitive-modifier + noun head); pins the synthetic-hyphen/case-casting rules
real word (international trade organization), 2 modifiers — at our engine's supported ceiling

TANTUM DEMOTION case: the 2-part split has a plurale-tantum head and BinPackage demotes it in favor of the 3-part split with a full-paradigm head. Our Compounds.swift explicitly does NOT port tantum demotion.
modern compound (AI + planning); modifier itself recursively decomposes (gervigreindar = gervi+greindar)
4-part real modern compound (AI data centers) — EXCEEDS our engine's 2-modifier ceiling
capitalized compound, directional modifier kept whole
all-uppercase 3-part compound (false prophets), split via head-chain recursion
real word (governing-coalition party), 2 genitive modifiers chained
4-part real compound (breakfast buffet); 'verðar' is the genitive of 'verður' unpacked from 'morgunverðar'
real word (school library) — canonical 3-part textbook example
real word (wine glass); possessive modifier 'víns' (gen. of víni) unpacked from 'rauðvíns'
real proper-noun compound (famous church); genitive personal-name modifier

decomposition: Vestur+Þýskaland (whole entry in BÍN, not OOV)
note: hyphenated proper compound (West Germany); NOT resolved by the compounder — it's a whole BÍN entry. Included as a boundary case: don't let compound logic re-split words BÍN already has whole.
Miðeind's own tokenizer gold-corpus stress word ('elf-well-bird-garden-the'); 4-part, deliberately at the edge of what their tokenizer must recognize as one token
OUT OF SCOPE for our single-word splitter: this is cross-token coordination ellipsis ('Finance and Economic Affairs Minister'), not a single-word compound. Category we have NO coverage for.
same coordination-ellipsis phenomenon ('bag and glove shop'); out of scope, no coverage

reason: non-Icelandic (Polish) word; DAWG lookup must return [] not crash or false-split

capitalized surface form of a genuine whole BÍN word ('æðarvarp', eider-duck nesting ground). Must resolve via the whole-word entry, NOT be split as æðar+varp. Guards against over-eager compounding on case variants of real words.
looks like a legal modifier+head join (margs+konar) but is NOT a legal Icelandic compound — correct spelling is always two words 'margs konar'. GreynirCorrect flags joining it as error C002.
three-part join of legal-looking pieces (aftur+á+bak) that is nonetheless not a real compound; must stay 'aftur á bak'
looks like modifier+head (annar+staðar) but the correct form is always two words 'annars staðar'
looks like a legal genitive-modifier + noun-head join but must stay 'mikils háttar'
same pattern as margskonar: 'sams konar' must stay two words

CONSTRUCTED, not a literal Miðeind test: 'mjög' is an adverb, excluded from BinPackage's _OPEN_CATS modifier/head restriction (no/so/lo only). Illustrates why closed-class words must never validate as compound parts even if morphologically stem-shaped.
source_repo: constructed from mideind/BinPackage design (dawgdictionary.py _OPEN_CATS, documented in our own Compounds.swift header)

reason: CONSTRUCTED: 'hestsins' is a DEFINITE (article-suffixed) genitive form; BinPackage's prefix DAWG and our isModifier() both exclude definite forms as linking modifiers ('hestsins' fails, 'hests' succeeds)
source_repo: constructed from mideind/BinPackage design, documented in our own Compounds.swift header

reason: CAUTION (see markdown report): attested BÍN inflected form present in BinPackage's suffix-removals.txt — Miðeind's shipped analyzer deliberately refuses it as a legal compound-head suffix (archaic/rare register). Illustrative sample only, NOT bulk-copied (127k-line source list) because the forms themselves are BÍN data (CC BY-SA 4.0), same redistribution caution as our own ATTRIBUTION.md.
